ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tests" (
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test */,
  "test_item" TEXT /* Name or identifier of the test item */,
  "test_type" TEXT /* Type of test (e.g., High Temperature, Low Temperature, High Temperature/Humidity) */,
  "test_category" TEXT /* Category of the test (e.g., Thermal, Electrical) */,
  "test_subcategory" TEXT /* Subcategory of the test (e.g., High Temperature Storage, Low Temperature Storage) */,
  "test_version" TEXT /* Version of the test */,
  "test_protocol" TEXT /* Detailed procedure for conducting the test */,
  "test_condition" TEXT /* Specific test condition (e.g., 70ºC 96hrs) */,
  "applicable_standard" TEXT /* Applicable standard for the test */,
  "test_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the test */,
  "test_duration_hours" INTEGER /* Duration of the test in hours */,
  "test_temperature_celsius" REAL /* Temperature in Celsius at which the test is conducted */,
  "test_humidity_percent" REAL /* Humidity percentage at which the test is conducted */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tests_applicable_standard FOREIGN KEY ("applicable_standard") REFERENCES applicable_standards ("standard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_conditions" (
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test condition */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test to which the condition applies */,
  "temperature_celsius" REAL /* Temperature in Celsius */,
  "humidity_percent" REAL /* Humidity percentage */,
  "duration_hours" INTEGER /* Duration of the test condition in hours */,
  "electrical_stress_voltage" REAL /* Electrical stress voltage applied during the test */,
  "electrical_stress_current" REAL /* Electrical stress current applied during the test */,
  "electrical_stress_waveform" TEXT /* Electrical stress waveform (e.g., Sine, Square) */,
  "electrical_stress_frequency" REAL /* Electrical stress frequency */,
  "condition_version" TEXT /* Version of the test condition */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_conditions_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES tests ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test result */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test for which the result is recorded */,
  "test_date" TEXT /* Date on which the test was conducted */,
  "test_result_status" TEXT /* Status of the test result (e.g., Pass, Fail, Inconclusive) */,
  "test_result_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the test result */,
  "test_result_version" TEXT /* Version of the test result */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES tests ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_equipment" (
  "equipment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each piece of equipment */,
  "equipment_name" TEXT /* Name of the equipment */,
  "equipment_type" TEXT /* Type of equipment (e.g., Temperature Chamber, Humidity Chamber) */,
  "equipment_model" TEXT /* Model of the equipment */,
  "equipment_description" TEXT /* Description of the equipment */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of last calibration */,
  "calibration_certificate_number" TEXT /* Calibration certificate number */,
  "maintenance_history" TEXT /* History of maintenance activities */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("equipment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_schedules" (
  "schedule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test schedule */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test being scheduled */,
  "scheduled_start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the test schedule */,
  "scheduled_end_date" TEXT /* End date of the test schedule */,
  "scheduled_time" TEXT /* Time of the day when the test is scheduled */,
  "equipment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the equipment used for the test */,
  "test_engineer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test engineer responsible for the test */,
  "schedule_status" TEXT /* Status of the test schedule (e.g., Scheduled, In Progress, Completed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("schedule_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_schedules_test_engineer_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_engineer_id") REFERENCES test_engineers ("engineer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_schedules_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES tests ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_schedules_equipment_id FOREIGN KEY ("equipment_id") REFERENCES test_equipment ("equipment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_engineers" (
  "engineer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test engineer */,
  "engineer_name" TEXT /* Full name of the test engineer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the test engineer */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the test engineer */,
  "specialization" TEXT /* Specialization of the test engineer (e.g., High Temperature Testing, Low Temperature Testing) */,
  "certification" TEXT /* Certification of the test engineer */,
  "training_records" TEXT /* Training records of the test engineer */,
  "availability" TEXT /* Availability of the test engineer (e.g., Available, Unavailable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engineer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "applicable_standards" (
  "standard_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each standard */,
  "standard_name" TEXT /* Name of the standard */,
  "standard_description" TEXT /* Description of the standard */,
  "standard_version" TEXT /* Version of the standard */,
  "revision_history" TEXT /* History of revisions to the standard */,
  "revision_notes" TEXT /* Notes about the revisions to the standard */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("standard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)
